steps.title: Use a restrictive name
steps.content: The sentence must identify which fellow OECD member nation is meant. "Australia" is essential information specifying the nation, so it should be placed directly after "nation" without punctuation that would mark it as a nonessential aside.

steps.title: Reject unnecessary marks
steps.content: B uses a colon after a phrase that is not an independent clause. C incorrectly uses a hyphen to attach a country name to a noun. D uses a comma, which would make the name seem nonessential even though it identifies the nation. The answer is A.
